summary: Russian UAVs were reported over multiple Ukrainian regions including Sumy, Kharkiv, Mykolaiv, Zaporizhzhia and Chernihiv. A ballistic missile struck Kyiv, causing fires and two casualties. Russian KABs hit Zaporizhzhia, wounding three including a child.
key_points: Russian UAVs were observed heading toward Sumy, Kharkiv, Mykolaiv and Zaporizhzhia from various directions (Source: @kpszsu/66636).
A ballistic missile attack on Kyiv caused fires in the Darnytskyi district, a residential building, an SТО and a non‑residential building, with two people injured (Source: @UkraineNow/82231).
Russian KABs were dropped on the Zaporizhzhia regional centre, resulting in three injuries, one of them a child (Source: @UkraineNow/82236).
UAVs were reported near the border of Sumy and Chernihiv regions moving southward (Source: @kpszsu/66638).
Multiple UAV alerts were posted for Kyiv, including a reactive Shahed and a ballistic missile warning (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68100, @mon1tor_ua/68143).
Gas stations in Kharkiv and Zaporizhzhia continue to be regularly attacked, according to a fuel expert (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31213).
UAVs were sighted over Mykolaiv heading toward Voznesensk and over Chernihiv heading west (Source: @kpszsu/66638).
UAV activity was reported near Kremenchuk and south of Reshetilivka in Dnipropetrovsk region (Source: @eRadarrua/98500).

sections.executive_summary: Russian UAVs were reported over Sumy, Kharkiv, Mykolaiv and Zaporizhzhia, indicating broad aerial activity. A ballistic missile strike on Kyiv caused multiple fires and two injuries. Russian KABs hit Zaporizhzhia, wounding three civilians, including a child.

points: Ukrainian authorities reported a ballistic missile strike in Kyiv's Darnytskyi district, igniting fires at several addresses, a residential building, an SТО and a non‑residential structure, with two civilians injured (Source: @UkraineNow/82231).

Fuel expert Leushkin stated that the gas station network in Trostyanets has been completely destroyed and that stations in Kharkiv and Zaporizhzhia are regularly attacked (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31213).
Ukrainian civil defense reported the return of seven civilians previously held by Russia, while estimating about 16,000 Ukrainian civilians remain in Russian captivity (Source: @UkraineNow/82230).
A Ukrainian man was arrested in Poland for online threats against President Nawrocki, facing possible imprisonment (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31216).
Ukraine's finance minister announced a need for nearly $146 billion in external financing for 2026‑2029 after meetings in Gdansk (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31224).
Russian KABs were dropped on the Zaporizhzhia regional centre, causing three injuries, including a child (Source: @UkraineNow/82236).
Multiple UAVs were reported over Mykolaiv heading toward Voznesensk and over Chernihiv heading west, indicating Russian aerial operations (Source: @kpszsu/66638).
Russian long‑range strikes on Ukrainian oil refineries and military plants were noted, with increased use of drone‑missiles and FABs (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31217).
Russian forces were described as using “Geranium” warheads on Ukrainian gas stations, leading to localized fuel shortages (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31217).
Russian officials discussed plans to increase escalation, including more frequent strikes on Kyiv (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31217).
UAVs were observed heading toward Sumy from the north and south, and toward Kharkiv from the north (Source: @kpszsu/66636).
A UAV was reported near the border of Sumy and Chernihiv moving southward (Source: @kpszsu/66638).
UAV activity was noted near Kremenchuk and south of Reshetilivka in Dnipropetrovsk region (Source: @eRadarrua/98500).
Three UAVs were reported over the Sumy region, including one near the city and another near the border with Chernihiv (Source: @kpszsu/66638).
UAVs were sighted near the Old Merchik area in Kharkiv region (Source: @eRadarrua/98520).
UAVs were reported in northern Chernihiv region heading west, and another near the border of Sumy and Chernihiv moving south (Source: @kpszsu/66638).
A UAV was observed on the north of Kyiv region passing near Chernobyl heading west (Source: @kpszsu/66641).
UAV activity was reported in the north of Chernihiv heading toward Snovsk (Source: @kpszsu/66669).
UAVs were reported in the north of Chernihiv heading west (Source: @kpszsu/66638).
A reactive Shahed was reported over Kyiv's Darnytskyi district (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68100).
UAVs were reported over southern Mykolaiv heading north, and another heading toward Zaporizhzhia from the south (Source: @kpszsu/66636).
A UAV was observed in Mykolaiv heading toward Voznesensk (Source: @kpszsu/66638).
UAV activity was noted near the southern Mykolaiv region with a northward course (Source: @kpszsu/66636).
UAVs were reported near the southern Mykolaiv region heading northward (Source: @kpszsu/66636).
A UAV was reported near the southern Mykolaiv region heading north (Source: @kpszsu/66636).
Russian UAVs were reported over multiple Ukrainian oblasts, including Sumy, Kharkiv, Mykolaiv, Zaporizhzhia and Chernihiv, indicating extensive aerial activity (Source: @kpszsu/66636, @kpszsu/66638).
A ballistic missile strike on Kyiv caused fires and two civilian injuries (Source: @UkraineNow/82231).
Russian KABs were dropped on Zaporizhzhia, wounding three civilians (Source: @UkraineNow/82236).
Ukrainian forces reported increased use of drone‑missiles and FABs against Russian targets, including oil refineries (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31217).
Reactive Shahed drones were reported over Kyiv and Darnytskyi district (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68100).

key_developments: Russian UAVs observed heading toward Sumy, Kharkiv, Mykolaiv and Zaporizhzhia from multiple directions, indicating coordinated aerial operations (Source: @kpszsu/66636).
Ballistic missile strike on Kyiv caused multiple fires and two civilian injuries in the Darnytskyi district (Source: @UkraineNow/82231).
Russian KABs hit Zaporizhzhia regional centre, wounding three civilians, including a child (Source: @UkraineNow/82236).
Ukrainian fuel infrastructure under repeated attack: gas stations in Trostyanets destroyed, Kharkiv and Zaporizhzhia stations regularly hit (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31213).
Increased Russian long‑range strikes using drone‑missiles and Geranium warheads on Ukrainian oil refineries and military plants (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31217).
Ukrainian civil defense reported return of seven civilians from Russian captivity, while estimating about 16,000 remain detained (Source: @UkraineNow/82230).
Ukrainian finance minister announced a need for nearly $146 billion external financing for 2026‑2029 (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31224).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian aerial activity remains widespread, with UAVs targeting key logistics hubs in Sumy, Kharkiv, Mykolaiv and Zaporizhzhia, and a ballistic missile strike on Kyiv demonstrating continued strike capability. The use of KABs and Geranium warheads against civilian infrastructure escalates the threat to Ukrainian energy and civilian sectors. Ukrainian counter‑strikes on Russian oil facilities suggest a reciprocal escalation, raising the risk of broader strategic strikes.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian authorities are reinforcing air defence in Kyiv and southern fronts, as indicated by alerts for Shahed drones and ballistic missiles. Local emergency services reported fires and casualties, prompting civilian evacuation and medical response. International partners continue to provide financial and military aid, though the scale of required financing remains high.
sections.intelligence_notes: UAV flight paths suggest coordinated launch points north of Sumy and south of Mykolaiv, possibly indicating staging areas in Russian‑occupied territories. The repeated targeting of fuel stations aligns with a strategy to disrupt Ukrainian logistics. Monitoring of KAB deployment in Zaporizhzhia is critical for anticipating further civilian casualties.
